name: Lion's Mane Extract
historicalUse: Lion's Mane mushroom has been used in Chinese and Japanese traditional medicine for centuries, appearing in the Shennong Bencao Jing and subsequent Chinese materia medica as a tonic for the stomach, nervous system, and mental vitality. Buddhist monks consumed it to enhance meditative clarity. It is classified in Chinese medicine as a shen tonic — a medicine that nourishes the spirit and calms the mind.
modernEvidence: Lion's Mane contains hericenones (from fruiting body) and erinacines (from mycelium) that stimulate Nerve Growth Factor (NGF) synthesis. Fruiting body extract is preferred for hericenone content. Human clinical trials demonstrate improvements in mild cognitive impairment, depression, anxiety, and sleep quality. Erinacines cross the blood-brain barrier and directly stimulate NGF in the CNS.
mechanisms: NGF stimulation — hericenones upregulate endogenous nerve growth factor synthesis
BDNF upregulation — promotes neuroplasticity and synaptic density
Myelination support — NGF-dependent myelin repair mechanisms
Neurogenesis — stimulates hippocampal neurogenesis in animal models
benefits: Supports healthy mood and mental clarity
Contributes to nerve health and neurological function
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. Fruiting body extract is preferred over mycelium-on-grain products (which contain significant starch content). Rare reports of skin itchiness in sensitive individuals.
contraindications: Caution in patients with known bleeding disorders at high doses
drugInteractions: Diabetes medications — beta-glucans have hypoglycemic properties; monitor glucose
Immunosuppressants — immune-modulating beta-glucans; theoretical concern in transplant patients
physicianNotes: Specify fruiting body extract standardized to beta-glucan content — mycelium-on-grain products have poor active compound density. Clinically appropriate in MCI, neuroprotective protocols, and cognitive aging support. Particularly promising for nerve regeneration support in peripheral neuropathy (ongoing research).
patientGuidance: Ensure you are taking fruiting body extract (as specified in this formulation) rather than mycelium powder, which is significantly less potent. Effects on cognition typically develop over 4–8 weeks.
